name: Sycamore
description: Fine-grained reactive library inspired by SolidJS, lean and signal-based

description.value: A library for creating reactive web apps in Rust and WebAssembly, powered by fine-grained reactivity without a virtual DOM
description.comment: Official tagline from the repo README and website.

license.comment: Workspace Cargo.toml and crates.io both list MIT only (not the common MIT/Apache dual).

documentation.comment: Official book at sycamore.dev/book; API docs at docs.rs/sycamore.

maintenance-status.comment: Commits as recent as 2026-06-15 (Edition 2024 migration, bug fixes); checked 2026-07-19. Note the last published crates.io release (0.9.2) is from 2025-09.

maturity.comment: Still pre-1.0 (0.9.2) with an evolving API (reactive primitives were reworked in 0.9); usable and established but not yet 1.0 stable.

corporate-foundation-backing.comment: No corporate/foundation sponsor; primarily driven by maintainer lukechu10 with community contributors.

reactivity-model.comment: Fine-grained reactivity via signals, inspired by SolidJS; explicitly avoids a virtual DOM.

view-authoring.comment: Custom view! macro DSL plus a Builder API (v2); both are type-checked at compile time.

fine-grained-updates.comment: Only the parts of the app that depend on a changed signal update; no full-subtree diff.

uses-virtual-dom.comment: Explicitly designed without a virtual DOM.

async-await-in-components.comment: Async components plus resources/suspense API with first-class async/await support.

global-state-solution.comment: Provide/inject contexts plus shared reactive signals.

primary-build-tool.comment: Trunk is the recommended toolchain; examples are built with Trunk.

official-cli.comment: No first-party CLI; relies on Trunk for scaffolding/build/dev-server.

dev-server-hmr.comment: Dev workflow via Trunk's auto-rebuild + browser refresh; no view/RSX hot reload.

minimal-wasm-bundle-size.comment: No reliably documented, methodically-measured hello-world gzipped .wasm size available; per RESEARCH.md, prefer null over an unmethodical number.

js-interop.comment: Built on wasm-bindgen; web-sys and js-sys used for browser/JS API access.

js-interop-ergonomics.comment: Standard wasm-bindgen path with some boilerplate; the dedicated JS interop doc page is explicitly marked incomplete.

no-std-minimal-runtime.comment: Depends on std / wasm-bindgen / web-sys; no no_std mode documented.

client-side-rendering-spa.comment: SPA mode works as the baseline; SSR is optional.

server-side-rendering.comment: render_to_string for server-side rendering, supported out of the box.

static-site-generation.comment: No first-party static-site-generation feature documented; render_to_string could be scripted at build time but there is no dedicated SSG tooling.

hydration.comment: hydrate/hydrate_to reuse existing DOM nodes; no islands or progressive/partial hydration documented.

server-functions.comment: No integrated isomorphic server-function mechanism (unlike Leptos); SSR is via render_to_string and manual server wiring.

streaming-ssr.comment: render_to_string_stream streams a fallback shell then updates as suspense boundaries resolve.

backend-integration.comment: SSR is framework-agnostic (render_to_string returns a string usable with any server); no first-party axum/actix integration crate.

isomorphic-routing.comment: The same sycamore-router runs on client (HistoryIntegration) and server for SSR.

official-router.comment: sycamore-router is maintained in-tree by the core team.

nested-routes.comment: Nested routes via catch-all segments feeding another Route-deriving enum.

dynamic-route-params.comment: Angle-bracket dynamic segments, including typed captures and <path..> variadic catch-all.

typed-routes.comment: Routes are a #[derive(Route)] enum, type-checked at compile time (e.g. a missing #[not_found] is a compile error).

route-guards.comment: No built-in navigation guards/redirect hooks documented; must be handled manually in the view closure.

lazy-code-split-routes.comment: No first-party lazy/code-split route loading documented.

styling-approach.comment: Not opinionated; raw CSS files and CSS frameworks via Trunk, with documented Tailwind integration. No built-in scoped or CSS-in-Rust system.

scoped-styles.comment: No built-in component-scoped styling; styling is delegated to external CSS.

component-library-ecosystem.comment: Essentially no mature ready-made UI component kits; users generally build their own or use CSS frameworks.

forms-inputs.comment: bind: directive two-way-binds signals to value/valueAsNumber/checked; can also be driven as controlled inputs.

svg-canvas-support.comment: SVG support in the view macro (dedicated svg example); canvas reachable via web-sys.

suspense-loading-ui.comment: Suspense boundaries for async resources, integrated with streaming SSR.

error-boundaries.comment: No dedicated error-boundary component documented in the book.

documentation-quality.comment: Structured book with guide/cookbook, tutorials, API docs, and a live example gallery; a few pages (JS interop, testing) are marked incomplete/WIP, so not a full 5.

api-ergonomics.comment: Idiomatic signal API; the 0.9 reactive-primitives rework made signals Copy and removed prior lifetime/scope friction.

compile-times.comment: Macro-based view DSL; comparable to peer macro-driven Rust web frameworks.

examples-count.comment: About 26 example projects in the repo (>20).

interactive-playground.comment: Live example gallery; no full REPL, but each example is hosted at examples.sycamore.dev/<name>.

testing-support.comment: The testing docs state a testing story is still work-in-progress; generic wasm-bindgen-test works but is not an official documented story.

crates-io-downloads.comment: All-time downloads of the top-level sycamore crate; checked 2026-07-19.

community-size.comment: Dedicated Discord, ~71 contributors, ~3.3k stars; smaller than Leptos/Yew, which the docs note have larger communities.

ecosystem-crates.comment: First-party router and SSR integrations exist, but few third-party component kits; leaner than the Yew/Leptos ecosystems.

notable-production-users.comment: No widely-documented production users found; avoiding tutorials/demos per guidance.

meta-framework-full-stack-kit.comment: No separate meta-framework/full-stack kit; SSR/hydration are built into the core library rather than a dedicated fullstack layer.
